attributes.description: Alerts when the broker persistent store utilization exceeds 90% (`activemq.store.utilization > 0.90`). Above this level the broker may halt accepting new persistent messages.
attributes.name: [ActiveMQ OTel] Persistent store utilization high

investigation_guide.blob:
## ActiveMQ persistent store utilization high

### What fired
`activemq.store.utilization` averaged above 0.90 over the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
The persistent store (KahaDB or JDBC-backed) is the durable buffer for messages that must survive broker restarts. When it nears capacity, ActiveMQ stops accepting new persistent messages — producers using `DeliveryMode.PERSISTENT` will block or fail.

### Triage
1. Broker Health dashboard: check trends on `activemq.store.utilization` and absolute `activemq.store.limit` / `activemq.disk.store_usage`.
2. Is the issue cluster-wide (data growth) or isolated (slow consumer leaving deep queues)?
3. Look at queue depth per destination — most often a handful of queues hold most of the persisted data.
4. Check for paused/stopped consumers that are preventing message dequeue.

### Remediation
- Clear backlog on deep queues by restoring consumer capacity.
- Expand the configured store size (`storeUsage/limit`).
- Add broker storage or rotate to tiered storage backends.

### Tuning
- `> 0.90` with a 15-minute window is conservative. Tighten to 0.80 for deployments with long response times on provisioning new storage.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-activemq.otel-*
// Store utilization is a gauge (0–1 ratio)
| WHERE activemq.store.utilization IS NOT NULL
| STATS max_util = MAX(AVG_OVER_TIME(activemq.store.utilization)) BY activemq.broker.name, host.name
// Alert when persistent store exceeds 90%; broker may halt accepting new persistent messages
| WHERE max_util > 0.90
| SORT max_util DESC
| LIMIT 10
